segments.text: In that hour the disciples came to Jesus, saying,
Who then is greatest in the Kingdom of Heaven?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Jesus called a little child to himself, and set him in the middle of them
and said,
Most certainly I tell you, unless you turn and become as little children, you will in no way enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever therefore humbles himself as this little child is the greatest in the Kingdom of Heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever receives one such little child in my name receives me,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: but whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him if a huge millstone were hung around his neck and that he were sunk in the depths of the sea.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Woe to the world because of occasions of stumbling! For it must be that the occasions come, but woe to that person through whom the occasion comes!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If your hand or your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off and cast it from you. It is better for you to enter into life maimed or crippled, rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into the eternal fire.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If your eye causes you to stumble, pluck it out and cast it from you. It is better for you to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into the Gehenna of fire.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: See that you don’t despise one of these little ones, for I tell you that in heaven their angels always see the face of my Father who is in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For the Son of Man came to save that which was lost.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “What do you think? If a man has one hundred sheep, and one of them goes astray, doesn’t he leave the ninety-nine, go to the mountains, and seek that which has gone astray?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If he finds it, most certainly I tell you, he rejoices over it more than over the ninety-nine which have not gone astray.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Even so it is not the will of your Father who is in heaven that one of these little ones should perish.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “If your brother sins against you, go, show him his fault between you and him alone. If he listens to you, you have gained back your brother.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But if he doesn’t listen, take one or two more with you, that at the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may be established.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If he refuses to listen to them, tell it to the assembly. If he refuses to hear the assembly also, let him be to you as a Gentile or a tax collector.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Most certainly I tell you, whatever things you bind on earth will have been bound in heaven, and whatever things you release on earth will have been released in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Again, assuredly I tell you, that if two of you will agree on earth concerning anything that they will ask, it will be done for them by my Father who is in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there I am in the middle of them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Peter came and said to him,
Lord, how often shall my brother sin against me, and I forgive him? Until seven times?
segments.speaker: Peter came and

segments.text: Jesus said to him,
I don’t tell you until seven times, but, until seventy times seven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore the Kingdom of Heaven is like a certain king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When he had begun to settle, one was brought to him who owed him ten thousand talents.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But because he couldn’t pay, his lord commanded him to be sold, with his wife, his children, and all that he had, and payment to be made.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The servant therefore fell down and knelt before him, saying, ‘Lord, have patience with me, and I will repay you all!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The lord of that servant, being moved with compassion, released him and forgave him the debt.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “But that servant went out and found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred denarii,  and he grabbed him and took him by the throat, saying, ‘Pay me what you owe!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “So his fellow servant fell down at his feet and begged him, saying, ‘Have patience with me, and I will repay you!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He would not, but went and cast him into prison until he should pay back that which was due.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So when his fellow servants saw what was done, they were exceedingly sorry, and came and told their lord all that was done.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then his lord called him in and said to him, ‘You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you begged me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Shouldn’t you also have had mercy on your fellow servant, even as I had mercy on you?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: His lord was angry, and delivered him to the tormentors until he should pay all that was due to him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So my heavenly Father will also do to you, if you don’t each forgive your brother from your hearts for his misdeeds.
segments.speaker: Jesus
